title: localization — compliance evidence
description: Machine-translation providers are subprocessors under GDPR Art. 28. Source strings often contain UI copy (public) but occasionally domain data — tenant admins are responsible for ensuring no PII passes through auto-translate.

controls.evidence: Machine translation calls only send the source string — no tenant metadata, user id, or other context. Redactor pipeline strips PII regex patterns from source text before dispatch (email, phone, credit card).
TenantErased cascades hard-delete of all Translation + TranslationJob rows. Subprocessor-side deletion request submitted where required (OpenAI, Google, DeepL all offer 24–72h purge SLAs).
See subprocessors.json. DPAs signed with each engaged provider. Tenant-level opt-in for auto_translate.
EU-region drivers preferred (DeepL EU, Google europe-west, OpenAI EU region). Configurable per tenant via localization.driver_region.
Driver API calls TLS 1.2+. API keys stored via env vars, never in DB.
Per-driver health probes + quality-score metrics + drift-detection reconciliation.
